title: Loop and uLoop Assembly Protocol
author: Bernardo Pollak
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yxnfxme

steps.instruction: The required concentration for each donor part in ng/μL is plasmid length / 100. That will yield a concentration of 15 fmol/µL. For the receiver plasmid the target concentration is length / 200, yielding 7.5 fmol/µL.

Add into a PCR tube: 1 µL of each donor part at 15 fmol/µL. 1 µL of receiver plasmid at 7.5 fmol/µL. Add water up to 5 µL (assemblies from L1 and above do not need adjustment since 5 plasmids are used)

Odd and even level assemblies use different formulations due to specific requirements of the restriction enzymes used. For odd levels (BsaI or BsaI-HFv2), 10x T4 DNA ligase buffer is used. For even levels (SapI), 10x Cutsmart buffer is used mixed with 10x T4 DNA ligase buffer in a 1:1 ratio. For BSA (bovine serum albumin), check stock concentration. NEB 'molecular biology grade BSA' comes at 20 mg/mL, depreciated NEB purified BSA comes at 10 mg/mL. Prepare 20 µl of 1 mg/mL diluted BSA in water solution. Thaw buffers at RT, then place on ice. IMPORTANT: Do not use BsaI-HF, that enzyme will NOT work with this reaction. Use either BsaI or BsaI-HFv2. Use aliquoted volumes of T4 Buffer since ATP and DTT will degrade over short amounts of time (less than a month) affecting the efficiency of the reaction. If low efficiency is seen, use double the restriction enzyme volumes.

Calculate the number of reactions to be performed and include an extra reaction to account for pipetting errors. Per reaction: Odd level BsaI-Ligase 2x master mix: %s %s Reagent Concentration Volume (µL) HPLC H20 3 T4 DNA ligase buffer 10X 1 Diluted BSA (Bovine Serum Albumin) 1 mg/mL 0.5 T4 DNA ligase 400 U/µL 0.25 BsaI 10 U/µL 0.25 Even level SapI-Ligase 2x master mix: %s %s Reagent Concentration Volume (µL) HPLC H20 1.5 Cutsmart buffer 10X 1 DTT 100 mM 1 ATP 10 mM 1 T4 DNA ligase 400 u/µL 0.25 SapI 10 U/µL 0.25 Mix by pipetting (enzymes in glycerol will be in the bottom of the tube). Then pipette from the top for accurate dispensing.

substeps.instruction: Calculate the number of reactions to be performed and include an extra reaction to account for pipetting errors. Per reaction: Odd level BsaI-Ligase 2x master mix: %s %s Reagent Concentration Volume (µL) HPLC H20 3 T4 DNA ligase buffer 10X 1 Diluted BSA (Bovine Serum Albumin) 1 mg/mL
Even level SapI-Ligase 2x master mix: %s %s Reagent Concentration Volume (µL) HPLC H20
Cutsmart buffer 10X 1 DTT 100 mM 1 ATP 10 mM 1 T4 DNA ligase 400 u/µL
Mix by pipetting (enzymes in glycerol will be in the bottom of the tube). Then pipette from the top for accurate dispensing.

steps.instruction: Add the 5 µL of the corresponding reaction master mix to the 5 µL of DNA mix and mix thoroughly by pipetting.

Place the reaction on a thermocycler (PCR machine). And run the following program: Step 1 - 37 ºC for 3 min Step 2 - 16 ºC for 4 min Repeat 25x step 1 and 2 Step 3 - 50 ºC - 5 min Step 4 - 80 ºC - 10 min Note: number of cycles and incubation times can be adjusted but the reaction efficiency may decrease.

Use 1 µL of reaction to transform chemically competent cells. Recover in SOC for 1 hour incubating at 37 ºC in a shaking incubator. Prepare plates with respective antibiotics with 50 ug/mL of X-GAL and 1 mM IPTG.

Pick 2 white colonies for each construction and grow in 10 mL of LB + respective antibiotic and perform plasmid purification. Validate assembly by means of restriction digest. Suggested enzymes: ThermoFischer XbaI and PstI FastDigest enzymes. If higher concentrations are required, increase inoculation volume and perform Midi prep as directed by manufacturer's instructions.
